content.problemStatement:
Create a simple Music Catalog system to demonstrate the Many-to-One relationship in Django.\
Create two models: Album and Track. Each track belongs to one album, but an album can have multiple tracks.

### Task
1. Complete the models.py file to create 2 models: 

i) `Album` with the following fields and attributes: \
         `title` - Char (max_length=200) \
         `artist` - Char (max_length=100) \
         `release_date` - Date 

ii) `Track` with the following fields and attributes: \
         `title` - Char (max_length=200) \
         `album` - ForeignKey(Album, on delete CASCADE) \
         `duration` - DurationField()

2. Complete the `homepage` function in views.py file to: \
i) Retrieve all the albums \
ii) Retrieve all the tracks

After completing the above, clicking on **RUN** will run migrations and start the server. The expected homepage should look like:
